description: Solr 10.x monitoring dashboard: node health, JVM performance, SolrCloud operations, index health, and cache efficiency. Use the environment and cluster dropdowns to scope panels to a specific deployment. Both default to All (matches all series) when labels are not configured.

panels.description: Rate of incoming query (read) requests per second. Spike without latency increase is normal scaling; combined spike indicates saturation.
panels.title: Query Request Rate

panels.description: Search request latency percentiles for /select handlers. Alert fires at p99 > 1000ms for 5 minutes (SolrHighSearchLatency).
panels.title: Search Latency p50 / p95 / p99

panels.description: Rate of incoming indexing (write/update) requests per second.
panels.title: Indexing Rate

panels.description: p99 latency for /update (indexing) requests. High latency may indicate index merge pressure or I/O bottlenecks.
panels.title: Update Latency p99

panels.description: Number of permanently loaded Solr cores across selected instances.
panels.title: Active Cores

panels.description: Percentage of disk space free on the Solr data directory mount (min across instances). Alert fires below 15%% for 5 minutes (SolrLowDiskSpace).
panels.title: Disk Free

panels.description: JVM heap memory currently in use per instance and memory pool. Uses max() to avoid double-counting the two OTel JVM instrumentation scopes (java8 + java17) emitted by Solr.
panels.title: Heap Used

panels.description: JVM heap memory committed (reserved from the OS) per instance and pool. Uses max() to avoid double-counting.
panels.title: Heap Committed

panels.description: Maximum JVM heap size (-Xmx) per instance.
panels.title: Heap Max

panels.description: p99 GC pause duration per collector and instance. Alert fires when total GC time > 10s/min for 3 minutes (SolrJvmGcThrashing).
panels.title: GC Pause p99

panels.description: GC collection frequency per collector and instance. Frequent major GC indicates memory pressure.
panels.title: GC Collection Rate

panels.description: JVM thread count by state per instance. Large BLOCKED or WAITING counts indicate lock contention or stalled I/O.
panels.title: JVM Threads

panels.description: Recent JVM CPU utilization (0–1) per instance. Sustained values near 1.0 indicate CPU saturation; combine with GC rate for root-cause analysis.
panels.title: JVM CPU Utilization

panels.description: Pending operations in the Overseer collection work queue. Alert fires > 50 for 5 minutes (SolrOverseerQueueBuildup). This metric is only emitted in SolrCloud mode.
panels.title: Overseer Collection Work Queue

panels.description: Pending cluster state updates in the Overseer queue. High values indicate ZooKeeper write pressure.
panels.title: Overseer State Update Queue

panels.description: Number of shard replicas where this instance is the current leader.
panels.title: Shard Leaders on Node

panels.description: Rate of ZooKeeper operations per instance. High rates may indicate excessive ZK polling or state update storms.
panels.title: ZooKeeper Ops Rate

panels.description: Number of transaction logs remaining to replay during leader recovery. Non-zero indicates a replica is catching up after restart.
panels.title: Update Log Replay Remaining

panels.description: Number of Lucene segments per core. High counts (> 50) degrade search performance; trigger explicit merges or tune mergeFactor.
panels.title: Segment Count per Core

panels.description: Total Lucene index size in megabytes across all selected cores.
panels.title: Total Index Size

panels.description: Percentage of available MMap address space (physical RAM minus heap) used by the index. Above 70%% (yellow) risks page-cache thrashing; above 85%% (red) causes I/O bottlenecks. Requires jvm_system_memory_total_bytes (Solr 10.x physical RAM metric). Shows "No data" if absent.
panels.title: MMap Efficiency

panels.description: Rate of IndexWriter segment flushes per second. High flush rates indicate heavy write load or small RAM buffer (solr.autoSoftCommitMaxDocs).
panels.title: Flush Rate

panels.description: Rate of completed minor (small segment) Lucene merge operations per second.
panels.title: Minor Merge Rate

panels.description: Rate of completed major (large segment) Lucene merge operations per second. Sustained major merges impact query latency; consider scheduling merges during off-peak hours.
panels.title: Major Merge Rate

panels.description: Total number of searchable documents per core. Tracks index growth over time.
panels.title: Documents Indexed

panels.description: Documents added but not yet committed and visible to searchers. High values indicate delayed hard commits.
panels.title: Pending Commit Docs

panels.description: Filter (DocSet) cache hit rate. Values below 80%% suggest the cache is too small or queries are too varied for effective caching; increase filterCacheSize in solrconfig.xml.
panels.title: Filter Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Query result cache hit rate. Caches complete result sets; high hit rates reduce CPU for repeated queries.
panels.title: Query Result Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Document (stored field) cache hit rate. Relevant when stored fields are frequently fetched during results hydration.
panels.title: Document Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Rate of filter cache evictions per second. Sustained evictions indicate the cache working set exceeds the configured max size.
panels.title: Filter Cache Evictions

panels.description: Rate of query result cache evictions per second. High eviction rates with low hit rates indicate insufficient cache size.
panels.title: Query Result Cache Evictions

panels.description: Rate of document cache evictions per second.
panels.title: Document Cache Evictions

panels.description: Total RAM consumed by each Solr cache type (filterCache, queryResultCache, documentCache) across all selected cores.
panels.title: Cache RAM Used

title: Solr 10.x Overview
